narrative: OMX30 opens Thursday in a strong bull regime with +8.3% positioning above the 200-MA and a robust ADX of 48.7 signaling sustained uptrend momentum. Continental European peers delivered solid overnight gains (CAC +0.56%, FTSE +0.43%), offsetting weakness in tech-heavy NASDAQ (-0.66%) and Asia ex-Hong Kong. Commodity strength—gold +0.47% and copper +0.52%—provides direct support for Boliden and broader industrials, while weaker DXY (-0.40%) and modest USD/SEK depreciation (-0.14%) favor export-oriented names. However, RSI at 70.97 and Stochastic K=82.3 indicate overbought conditions, creating mean reversion risk and warranting tactical caution despite the bullish setup. Watch today's US Jobless Claims (14:30 CET) as a potential volatility trigger.
morningBrief: OMX30 enters with tailwinds from Continental Europe's overnight strength and a bull market structure (+8.3% above 200-MA, MACD bullish). Industrials and exporters benefit from softer DXY and commodity support; copper +0.52% is a direct positive for Boliden and mining-adjacent industrial suppliers. Banks face a yield headwind with US 10Y Yield retreating -0.46%, though Swedish banking stocks can leverage tighter spreads vs. ECB rates. Key caution: RSI 70.97 and Stochastic overbought readings flag exhaustion risk; expect tactical pullbacks toward 3123 (yesterday close) or 3085 (lower Bollinger) if sellers emerge. Sectors to favor: Industrials (Volvo B, Atlas Copco A), Mining (Boliden), defensives (AstraZeneca, Essity B) on risk-off undertones from NASDAQ weakness. Watch US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET for direction confirmation.
drivers: Continental Europe outperformance (DAX +0.26%, CAC +0.56%, FTSE +0.43%) supporting OMX30 sentiment
OMX30 in strong bull regime: +8.3% above 200-MA, MACD above signal, ADX 48.7 (strong trend)
Commodity tailwinds: gold +0.47%, copper +0.52% benefiting mining & industrials
Softer USD/SEK and DXY -0.40% supports Nordic exporters
Overbought technicals (RSI 70.97, Stochastic K=82.3) signal mean reversion risk

sectorImpacts.reason: Gold +0.47% and copper +0.52% drive direct revenue uplift; Boliden is primary beneficiary.
Softer DXY (-0.40%) and EUR/SEK -0.13% support export margins; copper strength aids equipment demand signals.
US 10Y Yield fell -0.46% to 4.372%; Swedish mortgage and funding costs likely to compress short-term NIM expectations.
NASDAQ -0.66% overnight weighs on sentiment for growth-oriented tech names; Hexagon and Ericsson face DCF pressure from lower rates.
Defensive rotation play as NASDAQ underperforms and risk assets show overbought signals; flight-to-quality supports defensives.
Softer oil prices (-0.89%) provide modest tailwind to consumer spending; EUR/SEK weakness slightly pressures H&M sourcing.
Lower yield environment supports valuations, but refinancing stress persists; limited upside from falling US 10Y.

keyLevels.pivotWatch: 3123 (yesterday close; reference point for intraday directional bias)

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish economic releases today. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET is the primary catalyst; expected ~238K claims. Outcome may drive VIX and OMX30 volatility into London close.
topRisk: Overbought technicals (RSI 70.97, Stochastic K=82.3 D=85.6) create mean reversion pressure; sell-off toward 3123–3085 is likely if US data disappoint. Trade tensions (OpenAI/Trump headlines) could spike VIX (+1.21% overnight) and trigger deleveraging in crowded positions.
technicalSummary: MACD above signal with histogram +4.128 (trend strength intact)
ADX 48.7 with strong bullish DI bias (directional momentum robust)
Overbought on RSI (70.97) and Stochastic (K=82.3, D=85.6) — mean reversion risk
Bollinger %B=0.80 in upper half; 5.58% width suggests normal volatility
52-week: 96% of range, -0.8% from high (little room left to run without pullback)
